explanation.headline: T-Mobile reported a large 5G-NR 7/1 coverage increase in Charlie County but attributed most of it to existing towers — not new construction.
bullets: New coverage area: approximately 1238 km² (52% of the county).
100% of the new coverage is attributed to towers that already existed — the provider claims existing sites got stronger, not that new ones were built.
The county went from very little coverage to near-complete fill-in in one filing period.
explanation.recommendation: Schedule on-the-ground drive tests for 5G-NR 7/1 in Charlie County to confirm the reported coverage matches what consumers actually receive.

explanation.headline: T-Mobile reported a modest 5G-NR 7/1 coverage change in Delta County that does not raise major red flags.
bullets: New coverage area: approximately 4.42 km² (0% of the county).
100% of the new coverage is attributed to towers that already existed — the provider claims existing sites got stronger, not that new ones were built.
100% of new coverage hugs the county boundary rather than radiating from tower clusters — a pattern sometimes seen in map gaming.
explanation.recommendation: No immediate action required, but spot-check 5G-NR 7/1 in Delta County if resources allow.

explanation.headline: AT&T expanded 5G-NR 7/1 in Charlie County primarily through 1 new tower — a typical build pattern.
bullets: New coverage area: approximately 189 km² (8% of the county).
100% of growth comes from 1 newly inferred tower.
explanation.recommendation: No immediate action required, but spot-check 5G-NR 7/1 in Charlie County if resources allow.
